item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well done! I loved the S.T Joshi footnote - a very pleasant surprise

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is such a delightfully absurd entry, I absolutely love it. It's very deserving of more attention. The imagery of Foundation personnel in Antarctica packing C4 into mint cakes or a Shogoth sneaking into an explorer's camp to abscond with their mint cakes is amazing, the little scamp.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
More of this content on the wiki, please, anomalies that are internally consistent but aside from that make no dang sense. Why does it like gross mint cake? Who cares! It does, so let's make a monopoly.

Keep up the great work!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Aw, thanks! That’s super encouraging to hear. I appreciate you taking the time to comment.

I’m pleased that it’s got a positive vote total, let alone +50. That’s plenty high enough for me. I was sitting on this one for months before I posted it; I wasn’t expecting that it would be this well received.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I adore this. I found it through the tag golf challenge thread, and it's instantly become one of my favourites — and yes, it is underrated. I could see it climbing past +100, easily; it certainly deserves to.

Upvoted, and long may the Quiggan reign.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you. I absolutely agree. I feel like that’s the crux of it, and I’m not sure that I could’ve put it so succinctly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks Mark. No, my brain seems to use a ‘random article’ button of its own sometimes, so I figured I’d lean in to that.

I picked some elements with only the most tenuous of pre-existing associations, and contrived a kind of connective web to establish relationships between disparate elements.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
How gloriously free-associative. Did the idea come about from challenging yourself to hit the "random article" button in Wikipedia a couple of times and make a scip from combining the results? If so, do it again, because it's come out nicely.

Also I really want some mint cake now. Probably shouldn't have skipped dinner.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Minmin! Thank you. I’m hyped that you think this is underrated; it already has more upvotes than I expected.

item.title: Re: Called it!
item.content:encoded: Many thanks Fox! For your comment and for your crit and encouragement on this one. I appreciate it heaps.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Bumping this because it's underrated. This is the good kind of worldbuild that establishes crazy rules worth learning about in their own right, without being too overt or caught up in its own mechanics. ++

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
At The Mountains Of Comic Sans?

Honestly, I think we all missed that one. Good eye, SJolene!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Absurdism is not necessarily ever a bad thing in an SCP. As long as an article has a flowing internal logic cycle, it isn't random. It's not whether or not something is weird, it's whether or not it's consistent.

item.title: Forum Crit Junior Staff-Post - Info
item.content:encoded: Well, that's vague, serpentrose. If you're going to provide feedback, please elaborate on your feedback so the author can utilize it.

item.title: Called it!
item.content:encoded:
I told ya readers were gonna love it, Wax.

It's well balanced between charm and dread.

Have a well-deserved +1!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Shaggydredlocks! I’m pleased to hear that you liked it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I’m so glad that you found it entertaining. Thanks for commenting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hi there RockTeethMothEyes. Your crit was a great help, thanks again.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hey mayo! Thanks again for the crit and encouragement; you helped me muster the courage to post this article.

My concept was initially a sentimental tale about a child with a view of a mountain range from her bedroom window, and her overworked father always busy making graphs in his windowless office next door, with the analogy between the synchronised mountains and graphs symbolising their emotional connection. That idea mutated into this one during the drafting process.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh my gosh, how embarrassing. I really appreciate you taking the time to comment and point that mistake out to me, it’s extremely helpful!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hot shit, this is a real treat! Crazy original, and hilariously absurd. Love it! +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Glad I could help, but… I really think it should be topographic, not typographic, unless the anomaly changes the font of the mountains…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is an absolutely brilliant and absurd anomaly and I fell in love instantly. Excellent first skip, author!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So, I was responding to a thread where Waxwing asked for crits on a different draft in their sandbox. But I liked this article so much I dropped in a review of it simultaneously in the same post.

Shoggoths springboarding off a geomathematical weakness in the fabric of our reality in order to steal tasty minty cakes?! That is a glorious fusion of wacky hijinks and genuinely inventive eldritch horror that just tickles me right in the funny bone. And the dread bone. And… several other bones.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Silly Quiggan, mint cakes are for humans!
This is seriously good, you've weaved a lot of disparate concepts into a coherent and extremely original and entertaining whole.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks for the feedback. I’m not confident that it’s funny enough to be a -J, but if you feel that it’s too absurdist or whimsical for the mainlist I can see where you’re coming from.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks SJolene.

Yikes. You’re right- the terraforming aspect should be raised in the main description section. I’ll pop a sentence in there about it right now:

Following a procedural failure, the corresponding mountain area may demonstrate typographical alterations, maintaining continuity with the form of the synchronised graph.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is wacky and offbeat in exactly the right way. The idea that Kendal mint cake is inherently anomalous and requires the Foundation to monopolise its production, the concept of sales graphs correlating with mountain ranges to alter them, and especially "the phylum Eldritch" … it's all great, even if the monster itself is a bit bland by necessity.

I do think the Lovecraft connection feels kind of shoehorned, and the fact that the sales graphs can alter the topography isn't clearly established prior to the "Update" section, but on the whole this is a great entry.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hi ghosthorses. Thanks very much, that’s so kind of you to say. I’m glad you enjoyed the article.

I’d been trying to write drafts based on what I guessed readers would want to see, but was having limited success with that, so I just wrote this one based on what appeals to me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks Sirpudding! I only decided to make it that way as I was writing the paragraph. Originally an expedition was going to disappear or something, but then I remembered that Scott’s team starved trying to reach the South Pole and it clicked together. Ostensibly the expedition failed due to inadequate preparation, but, Occam’s razor- a Shoggoth probably made off with their food sled and went on a mint binge in the nearest crevasse.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'd prefer this as a -J, but other than that I've no complaints.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The bit where the expedition wasn't wiped out by hostile shoggoth guards or awakened from suspended animation elder things justifiably upset about waking up in a frozen waste dissected by apes, but because the shoggoth stole their food and scampered like a amphorous giant racoon is probably one of the best bits I have ever read here. This is a weird wonderful thing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh my gosh, this is such a delight… in exactly the way one of those mint cakes probably wouldn't be. I wanted to upvote so bad after this masterful line:

Directors of these companies will be responsible for ensuring that no metric of performance over time could feasibly result in the production of a line graph matching the form of any lengthy section of the Transantarctic Mountain Range, as viewed from any angle.

and the rest of the article didn't disappoint. I love the conceptual connections you've made, love the analogy between the mountain range silhouettes and growth charts, and the inclusion of Eldritch as a phylum is genius to me.

Emphatic +1, great first submission.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My first submission to the wiki. Please let me know what you think. Unless you vehemently hate it, in which case, shush!

Kendal mint cake isn’t proper cake, like a nice, light coffee and walnut sponge, or a strawberry gateaux; it’s just this weird, crumbly slab of sugar and glucose flavoured with peppermint, and sometimes covered in chocolate to hide it. I don’t like it very much, but I imagine it’s the kind of thing that a knock-off Shoggoth might be into..? Right?

Don’t even get me started on Azathoth and Curlywhirly bars.

Huge thanks to my wonderful reviewers: Kakroom, Reverend Fox, RockTeethMothEyes, and mayoculpa.

Also, thanks to everyone who reviewed my earlier attempts which I decided to shelf for the time being; They gave me a lot of great advice which (hopefully) prevented me making the same mistakes again in this article. Including (but not limited to) Jacob Conwell, DarkStuff, Eskobar, Zyn, UraniumEmpire, Roget, Meserach, and Rimple!

Image attribution:

“Quiggins-Kendal-mint-cake-factory” by Sladen. Sourced from Wikimedia Commons. This image is in the public domain.

ETA: Thanks to Accelerando for adding the tags, thanks to Shaggydredlocks for sharing the article on the Reddit, and thanks to ashausesall for translating the article into Chinese! That’s wild!
